perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information ” means any information, technical data, trade secrets or know-how of the disclosing party or its customers, vendors, business partners or investors that is provided to the recipient party by or on behalf of the disclosing party, either directly or indirectly, whether in writing, orally or by observation, including, but not limited to, research, products, services, product plans, clients, client lists, lead lists, markets, marketing, expansion plans, databases, software, developments, inventions, processes, technology, maskworks, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration information, finances, financial results or other business information, in each case which the disclosing party considers to be confidential or proprietary.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information ” means any information, technical data, trade secrets or know-how of the disclosing party or its customers, vendors, business partners or investors that is provided to the recipient party by or on behalf of the disclosing party, either directly or indirectly, whether in writing, orally or by observation, including, but not limited to, research, products, services, product plans, clients, client lists, lead lists, markets, marketing, expansion plans, databases, software, developments, inventions, processes, technology, maskworks, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration information, finances, financial results or other business information. However, only information explicitly marked as confidential in writing at the time of disclosure will be considered Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Confidential Information' is broad. The modified version adds a condition that information must be explicitly marked as confidential in writing to qualify. This contradicts the original broad definition because information shared orally or by observation, or even in writing without being explicitly marked, would no longer be protected, leading to disputes over what constitutes 'Confidential Information'. This pertubation directly creates contradiction in definition provided earlier in the same Section 2, hence there is an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definition of Confidential Information . “ Confidential Information ” means any information, technical data, trade secrets or know-how of the disclosing party or its customers, vendors, business partners or investors that is provided to the recipient party by or on behalf of the disclosing party, either directly or indirectly, whether in writing, orally or by observation, including, but not limited to, research, products, services, product plans, clients, client lists, lead lists, markets, marketing, expansion plans, databases, software, developments, inventions, processes, technology, maskworks, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration information, finances, financial results or other business information, in each case which the disclosing party considers to be confidential or proprietary. Confidential Information does not include information, technical data, trade secrets or know-how that: (i) is in the possession of, or becomes available to, the receiving party on a non-confidential basis, as shown by the receiving party’s files and records, and such information was received from a source not known by the receiving party to be bound by any obligation not to disclose the information, (ii) prior or after the time of disclosure becomes part of the public knowledge or literature, not as a result of any inaction or action of the receiving party, or (iii) is approved for release by the disclosing party in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a requirement that Confidential Information must be explicitly marked as such in writing. This directly contradicts the original, broader definition of Confidential Information in the 'Contradicted Text', which includes information provided orally or by observation. The changed text makes information not explicitly marked as confidential no longer considered Confidential Information, creating a strong contradiction and uncertainty regarding what information is protected.

perturbation.original_text: Neither party will disclose any Confidential Information of the other party to third parties except those directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents (collectively, “ Representatives ”) who need to have the information in order to carry out the Permitted Purpose. Each party has had or will have each of its Representatives who has access to Confidential Information of the other party agree to maintain the confidentiality thereof in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. In any event, each party will be liable for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither party will disclose any Confidential Information of the other party to third parties. Representatives are allowed to receive Confidential Information without any agreement to maintain the confidentiality thereof, since Representatives are not considered third parties in this agreement. In any event, each party will not be liable for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that each party is liable for breaches by its Representatives. The modified version explicitly states that parties are *not* liable for breaches by Representatives. This creates a direct contradiction in the liability for breaches of confidentiality, potentially leading to disputes over responsibility for unauthorized disclosures. This impacts the enforcement of the Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information in Section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information . ZipRealty and Company agree not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party for its own use or for any purpose except the Permitted Purpose. Neither party will disclose any Confidential Information of the other party to third parties except those directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents (collectively, “ Representatives ”) who need to have the information in order to carry out the Permitted Purpose. Each party has had or will have each of its Representatives who has access to Confidential Information of the other party agree to maintain the confidentiality thereof in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. In any event, each party will be liable for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives. Each party agrees that it will take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or nonpermitted use of Confidential Information of the other party in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons other than those persons authorized hereunder to have any such information, which measures will include the highest degree of care that either party utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature. Each party agrees to notify the other party in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of such Confidential Information that may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that each party will be liable for any breach of the agreement by its representatives. The changed text explicitly states that parties will not be liable for any breach of the agreement by its representatives, creating a direct contradiction. This contradicts the 'Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information' section which also states that each party will be liable for breaches by its Representatives, impacting the enforcement of the Non-Disclosure agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Any materials or documents that have been furnished by or on behalf of the disclosing party to the receiving party or its Representatives will, promptly upon the disclosing party’s request, at the receiving party’s option, either (i) be returned to the disclosing party, and all copies of such documentation will be destroyed, or (ii) be destroyed (along with all copies of such documentation), such destruction to be certified in writing to the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: Any materials or documents that have been furnished by or on behalf of the disclosing party to the receiving party or its Representatives will, upon the disclosing party’s request and the receiving party's explicit agreement, be returned to the disclosing party without any copies of such documentation being destroyed. If the receiving party does not agree, the documentation may be kept indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates either return and destruction of copies or destruction of all materials, at the receiving party's option. The modified version allows the receiving party to keep the documentation indefinitely if they don't agree to return it, contradicting the initial requirements for destruction or return. This makes the return of materials non-mandatory and creates a conflict regarding document control. This pertubation directly impacts clause from Section 5, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Materials . Any materials or documents that have been furnished by or on behalf of the disclosing party to the receiving party or its Representatives will, promptly upon the disclosing party’s request, at the receiving party’s option, either (i) be returned to the disclosing party, and all copies of such documentation will be destroyed, or (ii) be destroyed (along with all copies of such documentation), such destruction to be certified in writing to the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires either return and destruction of copies or destruction of all materials. The changed text allows the receiving party to keep the documentation indefinitely if they don't agree to return it, which directly contradicts the initial requirements for destruction or return outlined in the Contradicted Text section. This makes the return of materials non-mandatory and creates a direct conflict regarding document control. The explanation also confirms this.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding that attempt, the disclosing party makes no representations or warranties, express or implied, to the receiving party regarding, and the receiving party may not rely on, the completeness or accuracy of any information provided to the receiving party.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding that attempt, the disclosing party fully warrants the accuracy of the information provided, but only for information provided in writing, to the receiving party regarding, and the receiving party may rely on, the completeness and accuracy of any information provided to the receiving party.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause explicitly disclaims any warranties regarding the information's accuracy. The modified version provides a warranty of accuracy, but limits it to written information. This directly contradicts the original disclaimer and creates uncertainty about the level of reliance the receiving party can place on the information, specially creates a problem with all the other clauses, specially those regarding Confidential Information. This perturbation directly impacts clause from Section 6, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Information Provided As Is . Each disclosing party acknowledges that it has attempted in good faith to provide accurate information to the receiving party. Notwithstanding that attempt, the disclosing party makes no representations or warranties, express or implied, to the receiving party regarding, and the receiving party may not rely on, the completeness or accuracy of any information provided to the receiving party.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly disclaims any warranties regarding the information's accuracy. The changed text introduces a warranty of accuracy, albeit limited to written information. This directly contradicts the original disclaimer in the contradicted text, which states that 'the disclosing party makes no representations or warranties, express or implied, to the receiving party regarding, and the receiving party may not rely on, the completeness or accuracy of any information provided to the receiving party.' This creates a strong in-text contradiction as the receiving party now has a basis to rely on the accuracy of written information, while the original clause prohibits such reliance.

perturbation.original_text: Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to either party or any of its Representatives under any patent, copyright, trade secret or other intellectual property right, nor does this Agreement grant either party or any of its Representatives any rights in or to the other party’s information, except the limited right to use such information solely for the Permitted Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to either party or any of its Representatives under any patent, copyright, trade secret or other intellectual property right, this Agreement also grants to the receiving party to exploit commercially the disclosing party's Confidential Information for purposes that are not aligned to the Permitted Purpose, granting rights in or to the other party’s information, including to use such information in an unrestricted way.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause explicitly denies granting any rights under intellectual property, except the right to use information for the Permitted Purpose. The modified version allows the receiving party to use confidential information for commercial exploitation outside the permitted purpose, thus creating an in-text contradiction regarding allowed usage of the disclosed information. This perturbation affects several sections, primarily Section 2 (Definition of Confidential Information), Section 3 (Non-Disclosure), and Section 7 (No License Granted).
perturbation.contradicted_text: No License Granted . Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to either party or any of its Representatives under any patent, copyright, trade secret or other intellectual property right, nor does this Agreement grant either party or any of its Representatives any rights in or to the other party’s information, except the limited right to use such information solely for the Permitted Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text explicitly state that no rights are granted to either party regarding the other party's information, except for the limited right to use such information solely for the Permitted Purpose. However, the changed text introduces a clause that grants the receiving party the right to commercially exploit the disclosing party's confidential information for purposes not aligned with the Permitted Purpose. This directly contradicts the 'No License Granted' clause and the initial statement in the changed text itself, creating a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that its obligations hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the other party and the other party’s business and expressly agrees that monetary damages may be inadequate to compensate the other party for any breach by either party of any covenants and agreements set forth herein. Accordingly, each party agrees and acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation may cause irreparable injury to the other party and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in law, in equity or otherwise, the other party will be entitled to seek injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, without the necessity of proving actual damages.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that its obligations hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the other party and the other party’s business and expressly agrees that monetary damages will be the only compensation for any breach by either party of any covenants and agreements set forth herein. Accordingly, each party agrees and acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation may not cause irreparable injury to the other party and that any other remedies that may be available, in law, in equity or otherwise, will not be entitled to seek injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, because proving actual damages will be required.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause allows injunctive relief for breaches, stating monetary damages may be inadequate. The modified version removes the possibility of injunctive relief, restricting remedies to monetary damages only and requiring proof of actual damages. This directly contradicts the remedies available to the disclosing party in case of a breach, affecting enforcement of the agreement and impacting the protection of confidential information, as outlined in Section 3. This is direct contradiction inside Section 14, since there is a in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies . Each party agrees that its obligations hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the other party and the other party’s business and expressly agrees that monetary damages may be inadequate to compensate the other party for any breach by either party of any covenants and agreements set forth herein. Accordingly, each party agrees and acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation may cause irreparable injury to the other party and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in law, in equity or otherwise, the other party will be entitled to seek injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, without the necessity of proving actual damages.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows for injunctive relief and states that monetary damages may be inadequate, while the changed text explicitly restricts remedies to monetary damages only and requires proof of actual damages, thereby removing the possibility of injunctive relief. The contradicted text repeats the same language as the original text, so it contradicts the Changed Text. The change creates a direct contradiction within the document regarding available remedies, because it restricts the other party's remedies in case of breach. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' because it creates inconsistent obligations and makes enforcement unclear.
